chunks.text: Hi, the topic is quicksort. First of all let us understand what is the idea behind quicksort

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: on what basis it works. What is the base idea about it? So let us take an example. So the

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: idea is if suppose there are group of students in a class and a teacher asks them to arrange

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: themselves in the increasing order of their height. There are two options a teacher can

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: show their places like you go there and stand at the back you come here and stand in the

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: middle or front whatever it is. So the teacher can show places to the students. This is one

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: option. Second option is teacher can ask the student to arrange themselves. So every student

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: will find his place in the sorted order. So the faster method or quick method will be

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the one if the students are finding their place. So yes this is the idea of quicksort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see I have taken some icons here some picture here to show you. Now if I ask

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the class who is shortest in the class then I can know that my place is in the beginning

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I can simply go and stand in the beginning. Now if you are the tallest person in the class

chunks.text: or a student is tallest he knows that I am the tallest of all he will simply go and stand

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: at the back. Nobody has to help him and nobody has to show his place. Then the rest they

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: will arrange themselves. How they will arrange themselves? Like let us take this guy what

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: he will do? He will ask these guys that you are taller than me you better go at the back

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and he will ask that person that you are shorter than me you come in front. So others will

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: find their places by arranging each other. Like suppose I have to stand in the line

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have to stand in the queue so then I will see that where I should come. I will see that

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the person who is taller he should be at the back of me so let him go at the back and shorter

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: person should come in front and they will find my place. That is the idea of quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now I will write few numbers 10, 80, 90, 60, 30, 20. Which element is obviously sorted

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: in the list? This one 6, 3, 5, 4, 2, 8, 9. Which element seems to be sorted in one glance

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: if you see this element is sorted. How we can say that? That is largest and is at the

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: back. Rest of the numbers don't know whether they are sorted or not but that is sorted

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: sure. In the same way if I have the numbers 4, 6, 7, 10, 16, 12, 13, 14. Which element

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is greater than that element? Then that element is in sorted position. Rest of the elements

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: may or may not be sorted. So this is the idea of quick sort. Quick sort works on this one

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and that's how the name comes quick. So like students can quickly arrange themselves so

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that's how this is the quick method for sorting but this is not the fastest method of sorting.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The name is quick. Now let us look at the procedure of quick sort. Quick sort is a divide

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and conquer algorithm. It follows divide and conquer strategy. So it means it will

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: split the problem into sub problems and solve those sub problems. So let us see how it works.

chunks.text: So I will make initial setup first of all then I will show you the working. So the initial

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: a list. I have total 9 elements this place is empty I have taken it. Now I will add here

chunks.text: infinity that is maximum number. Infinity is not defined in computer so we take some

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: maximum integer. That will act as end of the list. Like in strings we have slash 0 null

chunks.text: Then first element I will select as pivot. So pivot is 10. So it means I want to find

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the sorted position of this 10. Where this 10 should come? 10 should come at a place

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: such that all the elements smaller than 10 should be on left side and all greater should

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: be on the right hand side. For that I should check if any greater number this side I will

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: send it on that side. If any smaller on that side I will bring it this side. So for doing

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: so I will take i starting from pivot and j starting from infinity. I will search for

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the elements which are greater than 10 that is pivot and j will search for the element

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: which are smaller than pivot. So that they can exchange the numbers. Alright. So i at

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: most it will stop at infinity j it will at most stop at pivot maximum. If it is not getting

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: anything it will at most it will stop at pivot. Now let us see the procedure. The procedure

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: what we will do now is called as partitioning procedure. First I will perform the procedure

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then I will write on the algorithm for partitioning. Let us start. See increment i until you find

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: an element greater than 10. So the next element is greater than 10 only. Decrement j until

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: you find the element smaller than or equal to pivot that is 10. Yes it is smaller. Exchange

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: them. 5 comes here 16 goes there. One swap. Continue. Increment i until you find a pivotal

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: element greater than pivotal element. 8 is it greater than pivotal? No 12 is it greater than

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: pivotal? Yes. Now decrement j until you get an element smaller than pivotal. This is smaller.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Interchange them. 9 comes here 12 goes there. Continue. i this is greater stop. Decrement j

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is smaller stop. 3 this side 15 that side. Continue. Is it greater than 10? Is it greater

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: than 10? Yes. i comes here. Decrement j is it smaller than 10? Yes this is smaller than 10.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Stop here. That's all. Don't interchange i and j element because i became greater than j. i has

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: crossed j. It means we found the position of pivot. What is the position? j. Wherever j is

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: pointing that is the position of pivot. So send that element here and take 10 as this position.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this is sorted. This list is not yet sorted and this list is not yet sorted. You can see that

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: all the elements on this side are smaller than 10 and all the elements on that side are greater

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: than 10 and these are not sorted. These are not sorted and even those are not sorted. We have to

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: sort them. Then who is sorted? 10 is sorted. Pivot is sorted. This is the partitioning position.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the partitioning position. This is how partitioning position works. I have shown you the

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: partitioning procedure which has worked on one list and found out the position of a pivot element.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now break this. Perform quick sort recursively. Perform quick sort recursively on either side.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's it. This is the partitioning method. This is followed by quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let me write on the piece of code on this one.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Partition. It takes low and high as a parameter.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what it does? Select first element as pivot. So pivot is a of low.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then i was starting from here. j was starting from here. So i will be at low and j will be at high.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what we did with i? I was incrementing until it finds an element greater than pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it was incrementing if the element is a smaller than or equal to pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Do i++ while a of i is less than or equal to pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And similarly decrement j until you get an element

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and similarly decrement j until you get an element

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: smaller than or equal to pivot while a of j is greater than pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So i will stop if it is getting any greater element and j will stop if it is getting any

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: smaller element. So i have written the termination condition here.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what to do? If i is on this side and j is on that side only then interchange the elements.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If i is less than j swap a of i with a of j.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is just one comparison. Then again continue i increment i and continue

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: decrementing j and compare them and swap. So this process has to be repeated.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So i will write down this whole thing in a loop. How long i should do this?

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: While i is less than j. This i should continue as long as i is less than j.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This i should continue as long as i is less than j.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If i became greater than j it will stop and here it should interchange swap a of low

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: with a of j. So swapping of pivot element should be done with j and return j.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is partitioning position. That's all. This is a partitioning algorithm.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How quick sort works? So this is just partitioning.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us see how quick sort works. Quick sort it will take low and high.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If low is less than high means at least there are two elements.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If so then it will call partition algorithm by passing low and high and that partitioning

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: algorithm will return j the position where the partitioning is done.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then it will perform quick sort on left hand side that is low to j and it will perform quick sort

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: on right hand side from j plus 1 to high. That's it. So it will perform this part

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: perform from low to j and this perform from j plus 1 from here to this one perform quick sort on 2.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now one thing i can show you here this is already sorted. Why do you include j?

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See right hand side list is having infinity then where is the infinity for left hand side list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this sorted element will help as an infinity act as an infinity for first list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is a small piece of recursive quick sort which is using this partitioning.

chunks.title: QuickSort Algorithm - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Partitioning was finding the position of pivot by taking i and j.
